q: According to Newton's second law, an object that is accelerating must have a ___ acting on it.
An object moving in a circle, such as a ball on the end of a string, must therefore have a force applied to it to keep it moving in the circle. This force is known as the ___.
For uniform circular motion (speed $v =$ constant), the acceleration is centripetal acceleration which is directed towards the ___ of the circle at any moment.
In the absence of this force, the object would not move along a circle but move in a ___ line, according to Newton's first law.
For an object to move in a circle, a force is needed to deviate from its straight line path and ___ towards the centre of the circle.
The tension in the string provides the required centripetal force for the ball to keep moving in ___ path.
If this tension force is normal to the direction of motion of a body moving at constant speed, this force causes the body to move in a ___ path.
An attractive force exists between the sun and the earth in a direction ___ to the path of the earth.
The Coulomb attractive force between the ___ and the electron acts as the required centripetal force for the electron to move around the nucleus.
When a car turns in a circular path or a curve, a ___ force exists between the road and the car tires.
When a car moves in a circular path which is inclined to the horizontal, the ___ force of the road acts normally to the car.
The ___ thrust (lifting force) acts normally to an aeroplane body.
On a flat road (unbanked road), this force is supplied by ___ between the tires and the road.
Banking of roads is raising the ___ edge of the curved road higher than the inner edge.
The angle that the inclined track makes with horizontal is called angle of ___.
The normal force $F_{N}$ exerted by a banked road is ___ to the road.

Centripetal force is the force acting towards the centre of the circular path around which the object is moving whereas the centrifugal force appears to act in a direction pointing ___ from the centre.
The person pulls the string to exert an ___ force on the ball.
There is a misconception that this reaction force is the outward-directed ___ force acting on the ball.
When the car moves around a curve the person feels an ___ force exerted on him.
This is the centrifugal force caused by the effect of ___.
Actually, the centrifugal force is not a ___ force.
It is an apparent or ___ force which is equal and opposite to the centripetal force.
A centrifuge is a piece of equipment that puts an object in rotation around a fixed ___.

In a laboratory centrifuge that uses sample test tubes, the radial acceleration causes denser particles to settle to the bottom of the tube, while low density substances rise to the top. This effect is known as ___ principle.
Centrifugal pump is a ___ machine which converts mechanical energy into hydraulic energy.
The rotating ___ moves water or other fluids by using centrifugal force.
The centrifugal action of a washing machine is used in spin ___ the clothes.
The centrifugal governor is a device used to measure and regulate the ___ of a machine, such as an engine.
The centrifugal governors consist of two ___ of equal mass, which are attached to the arms.
